zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Jane Janet Hepburn於1482年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾波維爾時，她的父親Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell31歲，母親Lady Janet Douglas21歲。她於1507年1月25日與George Seton在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾西頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1558年5月10日在蘇格蘭艾丁波享年76歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾特藍特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Jane Janet Hepburn于1482年出生在苏格兰藍納可爾波維爾时，她的父亲Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell31岁，母亲Lady Janet Douglas21岁。她于1507年1月25日与George Seton在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾西頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1558年5月10日在苏格兰艾丁波享年76岁，埋葬在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾特藍特。

nameMeanings.name: Hepburn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Durham) and Scottish: habitational name from Hepburn in Chillingham (Northumberland), Hebron (Northumberland), or Hebburn in Jarrow (Durham). The placenames probably all derive from Old English hēah ‘high’ + byrgen ‘burial place, tumulus’. Compare Hebron .

variants.name: Eborn
meanings: English (Middlesex, Buckinghamshire, and Oxfordshire): variant of Eburne, itself a possible variant of Hepburn . However, the placename Hepburn comes from northeastern England, and the H- spellings are a late development, which reinforces the suspicion that the name may have a different but unknown derivation.

variants.name: Hebron
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Hebron (Northumberland), possibly from Old English hēah ‘high’ + byrgen ‘burial place, tumulus’. See also Hepburn .
Czech: from the Biblical placename (II Samuel 2: 1-3), said to mean ‘alliance’ in Hebrew.

variants.name: Heyburn
meanings: English: habitational name from a lost or unidentified place, probably in southern England, named with an unknown first element + Old English burna ‘spring, stream’.
Irish (Ulster): possibly a variant of Scottish   Hepburn  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Janet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

variants.name: Jannet
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Jane Janet Hepburn was born in 1482, in Bothwell, Lanarkshire, Scotland, her father, Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell, was 31 and her mother, Lady Janet Douglas, was 21. She married George Seton on 25 January 1507, in Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 10 May 1558, in Edinburgh, Scotland, at the age of 76, and was buried in Tranent, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Jane Janet Hepburn est née en 1482, à Bothwell, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Janet Douglas, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé George Seton le 25 janvier 1507, à Seton, Haddingtonshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 10 mai 1558, à Edinburgh, Écosse, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Tranent, Haddingtonshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Jane Janet Hepburn 1482 in Bothwell, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell 31 und ihre Mutter Lady Janet Douglas 21. Sie hat am 25. Januar 1507 in Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland George Seton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 10. Mai 1558 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Edinburgh, Scotland und wurde in Tranent, Haddingtonshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Jane Janet Hepburn è nata il nel 1482 nel Bothwell, Lanarkshire, Scotland, sua padre, Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Lady Janet Douglas, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato George Seton il 25 gennaio 1507, nel Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 10 maggio 1558, nel Edinburgh, Scotland, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolta a Tranent, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Janet Hepburnが1482年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアボスウエルで生まれた時，父Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwellは31歳，母Lady Janet Douglasは21歳であった。1507年1月25日にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアセトンでGeorge Setonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1558年5月10日にスコットランドエディンブルグで76歳で死去し，スコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアトランエントにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Janet Hepburn(이)가 1482년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 벗웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Janet Douglas(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1507년 1월 25일 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 세턴에서 George Seton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1558년 5월 10일 스컷랜드 에딘버그에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 트랜트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Jane Janet Hepburn nasceu em 1482, em Bothwell, Lanarkshire, Scotland, seu pai, Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Janet Douglas, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com George Seton em 25 de janeiro de 1507, em Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 10 de maio de 1558, em Edinburgh, Scotland, com 76 anos, e foi sepultada em Tranent, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Jane Janet Hepburn родилась в 1482 г. в местности ботвелл, ланаркшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Janet Douglas, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Seton 25 января 1507 г. в местности сетон, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 10 мая 1558 г. в местности эдинбург, Шотландия в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности транент, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Jane Janet Hepburn nació en 1482, en Bothwell, Lanarkshire, Scotland, su papá, Patrick Hepburn 1st Earl of Bothwell, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Janet Douglas, tenía 21 años. Se casó con George Seton el 25 de enero de 1507, en Seton, Haddingtonshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 10 de mayo de 1558, en Edinburgh, Scotland, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultada en Tranent, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.
